selected_handles.why_relevant: Fresh structured guidance is available; treat it as context unless its contract says it is a verified commit.
Fresh structured guidance is available; treat it as context unless its contract says it is a verified commit.

frontier_blockers.effect: Residual statements remain after the live call frontier; their effects are not part of the callee obligation.
frontier_blockers.right: no right-side residual statement after this call frontier

limitations: The tactic shape is hidden until the handle is callable at the current frontier.
named_handles.why_visible: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.

residual_frontier.effect: These statements remain after the live call frontier and are checked after the call returns.
limitations: The surface reports suffix structure only; it does not choose whether to use a call, inline, wp, or another local tactic.

rows.proof_read: The left side has residual statements after the live call frontier; their effects are not part of the callee obligation.
rows.right: no right-side residual statement after this call frontier

moves.applicability: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.
moves.tactic_shape: Probabilistic VC plan: loop bad-event loss accounting via byequiv/bad-event bridge, event preservation, while invariant, rcond/case split

moves.applicability: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.

lines: Context : hr: {b : bool, i : int, n : nonce, ns, ns1, ns2 : nonce list,
ns1 = filter (fun (n0 : nonce) => (n0, C.ofintd 0) \in ROout.m) ns /\
ns2 = filter (fun (n0 : nonce) => (n0, C.ofintd 0) \notin ROout.m) ns /\
UF.forged = false /\ RO.m = empty<:nonce * C.counter, poly_in>) /\
(1.3)    UF.forged <- UF.forged || test_poly_in n Mem.lc r (oget UFCMA.log.[n])

source.label: Parsed from EasyCrypt pretty goal text

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

current_boundary_carries.tactic: seq 6 : (UFCMA.bad2 = false /\ UF.forged = false /\ ROIN.RO.m = empty) 1%r (qdec%r * maxr pr_zeropol pr1_poly_out) 0%r 1%r.

frame_obligation_ledger.interpretation: This ledger compares visible frame facts with the frame facts carried by committed structural boundary assertions. Possible drops are reported only for facts visible in the current local goal evidence. It is evidence about retained facts, not a proof script.

possibly_dropped.boundary_tactic: seq 6 : (UFCMA.bad2 = false /\ UF.forged = false /\ ROIN.RO.m = empty) 1%r (qdec%r * maxr pr_zeropol pr1_poly_out) 0%r 1%r.
possibly_dropped.evidence: required fact is visible in the listed source(s), while this boundary assertion does not carry the same frame fact

related_checkpoint.committed_tactic: seq 6 : (UFCMA.bad2 = false /\ UF.forged = false /\ ROIN.RO.m = empty) 1%r (qdec%r * maxr pr_zeropol pr1_poly_out) 0%r 1%r.
related_checkpoint.label: Before product-budget seq cut #8
related_checkpoint.why_here: Frame fact is visible in required context and absent from this structural boundary assertion.

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.

ask_manager_for.returns: call-ready/oracle-equiv context for the current frontier, plus the BODY of the procedure(s) called here (functor aliases resolved) so you can see which oracles/state your call invariant must preserve
ask_manager_for.use_when: The visible frontier contains call sites or named equiv handles may apply.

ask_manager_for.returns: the mechanical `={glob ...}` frame of the call invariant (shared abstract oracle modules, daemon-confirmed to apply and spawn the obligations). When a named coupling predicate fits the in-scope state it ALSO surfaces a field-threaded carrier (`inv … s1{1} s2{2}`), plus candidate inductive side-conditions (`dom ⊆ used-set`) for any random oracle the call queries. All revisable starting points — confirm the coupling and add/adjust the semantic conjuncts (key/state correspondences, set-membership).
ask_manager_for.use_when: You are at an abstract-adversary `call (_: <inv>)` and want the mechanical glob frame of the invariant before adding your own semantic conjuncts.

ask_manager_for.returns: a read-only preview of the obligations caused by that candidate invariant, including pre/init, call-preservation, oracle-side, postcondition, missing-fact signals, and extra conjuncts that may create avoidable obligations
ask_manager_for.use_when: You are considering `call (_: Inv)` and already have a concrete invariant expression you want to test first.

ask_manager_for.returns: valid tactic forms and common traps
ask_manager_for.use_when: A tactic has multiple EasyCrypt argument forms.
The frontier may need indexed `sp i j` before branch or call tactics.

ask_manager_for.returns: valid indexed `wp` forms and suffix traps
ask_manager_for.use_when: Mid-proof suffix may need indexed `wp` to absorb a tail before a cut.
Statement order may need a small `swap` before `sp` or `wp`.
A guarded branch may need `rcondt` after a case split or invariant fact.
A guarded branch may need `rcondf` after a case split or invariant fact.
A suffix proof may need `conseq` to weaken the pre/postcondition before closing.
A sampling step may need `rnd` to reduce it to a distribution/probability fact.

ask_manager_for.returns: swap/alignment context for the visible pRHL frontier
ask_manager_for.use_when: LHS/RHS statement order may need swap/alignment context.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

first_instruction_alignment.proof_read: The visible frontier is one-sided; use a one-sided tactic, alignment step, or seq cut before applying a symmetric tactic.
first_instruction_alignment.right_first: no matching right-side call at this frontier

rows.proof_read: This is a call frontier; compare the procedures/oracle wrappers before choosing a named call or invariant call.
rows.right: no matching right-side call at this frontier

rows.proof_read: This is a call frontier; compare the procedures/oracle wrappers before choosing a named call or invariant call.
rows.right: no matching right-side call at this frontier

rows.proof_read: The left side has residual statements after the live call frontier; their effects are not part of the callee obligation.
rows.right: no right-side residual statement after this call frontier

rows.proof_read: This is the loop frontier; choose a while invariant that carries the state needed by the postcondition.
rows.right: no matching right-side loop at this frontier

rows.proof_read: This is the loop frontier; choose a while invariant that carries the state needed by the postcondition.
rows.right: no matching right-side loop at this frontier

frontier_alignment.summary: Frontier alignment: loop guards are part of the current proof shape; call sites are visible at the frontier; residual statements remain after a call frontier. Use this as a proof-reader map before choosing while/rnd/seq/call tactics.

procedure_navigation.how_to_use: Positions for choosing wp/sp depth, which `if` to rcond, and where the next loop/call frontier is. Facts only — pick the tactic and the branch direction yourself.

items: current evidence points at a structural recovery boundary

evidence: frame_obligation_ledger reports a frame fact visible in the current local goal evidence
a committed structural boundary does not carry the same frame fact
limitations: does not infer a replacement invariant, cut formula, or postcondition
uses only current residual evidence and recent local repair failures

reference.note: Panels collapsed for focus. The essentials for THIS phase are in the focus above; the rest were noise here (a flat view of all of them is what disorients). If you genuinely need a precise field from one, ask with `inspect_context`.
